name: Media.Site
comment:
This package includes representations of various social media sites.

@docs all, image, video

values.name: image
values.comment:
Sites that are for image sharing. Includes:

- [Gfycat](//gfycat.com)
- [Imgur](//imgur.com)

values.name: video
values.comment:
Sites that are for watching videos/streams. Includes:

- [LiveCap](//livecap.tv)
- [Oddshot](//oddshot.tv)
- [Twitch](//twitch.tv)
- [YouTube](//youtube.com)

name: Media
comment:
This package is for extracting social media references from text. The
most common use-case for this package is to embed social media widgets on
a webpage from user input.

Basic example:

    import Media
    import Media.Site as Site

    text = """https://imgur.com/cjCGCNH
    https://youtu.be/oYk8CKH7OhE
    https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DfLvDFxcAIA
    """

    -- Find all the media references in some text
    media = Media.find Site.all text

    -- Generate URLs for media objects
    urls = Media.urls media

# Types
@docs Kind, Id, Media, SiteId, Site, Url, Urls

# Extracting media
@docs find, urls

unions.name: Kind
unions.comment: Represents the general type of media.

aliases.name: Media
aliases.comment: A slim reference to media on a site.
aliases.type: { id : Id, kind : Kind, siteId : SiteId }

aliases.name: Site
aliases.type: { id : SiteId, name : String, matchers : List ( Kind, String ), url : Media -> Maybe Url, imgSmUrl : Maybe (Media -> Maybe Url), imgMdUrl : Maybe (Media -> Maybe Url), imgLgUrl : Maybe (Media -> Maybe Url), iframeUrl : Maybe (Media -> Maybe Url) }

aliases.name: Url
aliases.comment: A basic representation of a URL.

aliases.name: Urls
aliases.comment: Represents all possible URLs for a given media object and site.
aliases.type: { media : Media, url : Maybe Url, imgSmUrl : Maybe Url, imgMdUrl : Maybe Url, imgLgUrl : Maybe Url, iframeUrl : Maybe Url }

values.name: find
values.comment: Find media links from input text based on a list of available sites.
values.type: List Site -> String -> List ( Site, Media )

values.name: urls
values.type: List ( Site, Media ) -> List ( Site, Media, Urls )
